# SecureChat

> An end-to-end encrypted chat system that uses a wallet-style X25519
> keypair as the user's identity. The server is a stateless relay that
> only ever sees ciphertext and routing metadata.

SecureChat is a small, auditable demonstration of how to build E2E
encrypted messaging without a server-side identity provider. It is
designed to be readable end-to-end in a single sitting (~1,500 lines).

## Design in one sentence

Each user generates an X25519 keypair in the browser; messages are
encrypted with NaCl `box` (X25519 + XSalsa20 + Poly1305); the server
relays opaque ciphertext and cannot decrypt anything.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): Quickstart, architecture, wire protocol,
  configuration, FAQ.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Threat model, cryptographic primitives,
  intentional non-goals, and reporting guidance.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md): Module-by-module reference.
- [.repobility/access.yml](.repobility/access.yml): Endpoint-by-endpoint
  authorization matrix.

## Source files

- `server.js`: Express + Socket.IO relay. Validates payload shapes,
  routes to recipient sockets, queues offline messages, broadcasts
  presence. No persistent storage.
- `public/crypto-utils.js`: Wallet keygen, encrypt/decrypt, validation,
  fingerprint, deterministic avatar.
- `public/app.js`: Wallet management, contacts, chat thread, presence,
  typing indicators, encrypted send/receive.
- `public/index.html`: Single-page UI with strict Content-Security-Policy
  (`script-src 'self'`, no third-party origins).
- `tests/`: Node native test runner suites — crypto roundtrip,
  authentication invariants, server validation, offline queueing.

## How to run

    npm install
    npm start
    # open http://127.0.0.1:3000 in two browsers
